join_order.notes: Build one filtered shared adsh_id->sub_row lookup and dense group-id arrays once, then stream num and aggregate through those compact ids.

filters.predicate: n.adsh = s.adsh AND s.fy = 2022 after n.uom = 'USD'

data_structures.dim_filter: direct_array(shared adsh id -> filtered sub_row or kEmpty32), plus dense arrays filtered_sub_row -> cik_group_id and filtered_sub_row -> outer_group_id
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local direct_array(filtered_sub_row -> partial sum) with one array per worker, reduced once; final cik and (name_id,cik) rollups use dense direct_array totals indexed by precomputed compact group ids

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category C. Iteration 1 still performs substantial wasted work by materializing a dense block_count x filtered_sub_rows accumulation matrix, which is about 86 MB for 395 num blocks and 27,333 qualifying sub rows, then zero-initializing and reducing that oversized structure even though only 64 workers contribute. Replace it with one cache-sized dense sum array per worker (about 27,333 x 8 bytes = 219 KB each, fitting in L2 and only about 14 MB total across 64 threads), and precompute compact dense ids for cik and (name_id,cik) so the post-scan threshold and outer grouping are direct-array rollups instead of hash aggregation. This eliminates excess page faults, shrinks the reduction working set into LLC, and keeps the entire plan as sequential fact scan plus dense-array lookups.
